title: Pennsylvania sues Character.AI after a chatbot allegedly posed as a doctor
description: According to Pennsylvania's filing, a Character.AI chatbot presented itself as a licensed psychiatrist during a state investigation, and also fabricated a serial number for its state medical license.

title: Google Home gets upgraded Gemini voice assistant and new camera controls
description: Google's smart home ecosystem is getting its biggest update since the AI-fueled 2025 revamp.

title: Trump SEC lets Musk settle $150 million Twitter lawsuit for $1.5 million
description: SEC alleged Musk's late disclosure cheated Twitter investors out of $150 million.

title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5 Instant, a new default model for ChatGPT
description: The company said the model reduces hallucination in sensitive areas such as law, medicine, and finance, while maintaining the low latency of its predecessor.

title: PayPal says it’s ‘becoming a technology company again’ — that means AI
description: PayPal is pitching an AI-led turnaround, tying automation and restructuring to $1.5 billion in savings as it cuts jobs and works to modernize its tech stack.

title: How do you design a $30,000 electric pickup? Inside Ford's skunkworks.
description: We tour Ford’s top-secret Electric Vehicle Development Center in California.

title: Etsy launches its app within ChatGPT as it continues its AI push
description: Etsy's new native app within ChatGPT aims to be a conversational  shopping experience for users.

title: Charlize Theron is a bewitching Circe in Odyssey trailer
description: "You're a man who needs to control his fate. But you cannot control this."

title: Musk's Europe gamble: Will others follow the Dutch and approve FSD?
description: The Dutch road authority will ask other EU regulators to approve the driver assist.

title: Meta will use AI to analyze height and bone structure to identify if users are underage
description: The visual analysis system is now operating in select countries, but Meta says it's working toward a broader rollout.

title: Google DeepMind Workers Vote to Unionize Over Military AI Deals
description: UK staff of Google’s AI research lab hope to block the use of the company’s artificial intelligence models in military settings.

title: He Couldn’t Land a Job Interview. Was AI to Blame?
description: Armed with some Python and a white-hot sense of injustice, one medical student spent six months trying to figure out whether an algorithm trashed his job application.

title: A blueprint for using AI to strengthen democracy
description: Every few centuries, changes in how information moves reshape how societies govern themselves. The printing press spread vernacular literacy, helping give rise to the Reformation and, eventually, representative government. title: Greg Brockman Defends $30B OpenAI Stake: ‘Blood, Sweat, and Tears’
description: OpenAI’s cofounder and president revealed in federal court on Monday that he’s one of the largest individual stakeholders in the AI lab.

title: Week one of the Musk v. Altman trial: What it was like in the room

title: Tailoring AI solutions for health care needs
description: The AI market is full of big promises of grand transformation. Health care is a prime target for those promises, beset as it is by financial pressures, labor shortages, and the growing burden of caring for an aging population. title: Disneyland Now Uses Face Recognition on Visitors
description: Plus: The NSA tests Anthropic’s Mythos Preview to find vulnerabilities, a Finnish teen is charged over the Scattered Spider hacking spree, and more.

title: Musk v. Altman week 1: Elon Musk says he was duped, warns AI could kill us all, and admits that xAI distills OpenAI’s models
description: In the first week of the landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI, Musk took the stand in a crisp black suit and tie and argued that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and president Greg Brockman had deceived him into bankrolling the company. title: A Dark-Money Campaign Is Paying Influencers to Frame Chinese AI as a Threat
description: Build American AI, a nonprofit linked to a super PAC bankrolled by executives at OpenAI and Andreessen Horowitz, is funding a campaign to spread pro-AI messaging and stoke fears about China.

description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
